editor_note: Faithful, native technical prose with consistent informal "tu"; core terms correct/consistent (backend kept as-is, transpilationвҶ’transpilation, coupling map/CouplingMap preserved, "erreur de lecture"/"longueur de lecture" for readout error/length). Nuanced claims survive intact вҖ” e.g. the caveat that coupling_map "peut ne reprГ©senter qu'un sous-ensemble" of the Target, and the version-vs-backend_version distinction ("version de l'interface abstraite Backend qu'implГ©mente l'objet" vs "mГ©tadonnГ©e sur la version du backend lui-mГӘme") both match the English precisely.

Meaning is faithful throughout: definitions (Г©tat classique, vecteur de probabilitГ©, matrice stochastique), the deterministic/probabilistic operation split, associativity vs non-commutativity, and the closing example all match the source with no drift or hallucination. Two mild issues. (1) The coin analogy localizes "heads/tails" as "pile/face" but backwards from standard French idiom: line 126 renders "heads first, tails second" as "pile en premier, face en second" (headsвҶ’pile, tailsвҶ’face), and line 135 renders "tails" as "face." Conventional French is face=heads, pile=tails, so a learner's default intuition is contradicted; it stays internally consistent and the math kets remain in English ($\vert\text{heads}\rangle$, $\vert\text{tails}\rangle$), so no mathematical contradiction, but it can momentarily confuse. (2) "inner product" is rendered as the calque "produit intГ©rieur" (lines 357-358) rather than the conventional "produit scalaire"; used consistently and not misleading. Everything else reads naturally and teaches as well as the English.

examples.source: Up to the ordering of the prime factors, there is only one prime factorization for each positive integer NвүҘ2
examples.translation: Hangga't iisa ang ayos ng mga prime factor, may iisang prime factorization lamang para sa bawat positibong integer na NвүҘ2
examples.suggested: Hindi alintana ang pagkakasunod-sunod ng mga prime factor, iisa lamang ang prime factorization para sa bawat positibong integer na NвүҘ2
examples.why: 'Up to the ordering' means uniqueness holds when order is disregarded; 'Hangga't iisa ang ayos' turns it into a precondition ('as long as the order is the same'), a subtle reframing of the mathematical claim.

examples.source: the product of 2 to the power 2 and 3 to the power 1
examples.translation: ang produkto ng 2 sa kapangyarihang 2 at 3 sa kapangyarihang 1
examples.suggested: ang produkto ng 2 sa eksponenteng 2 at 3 sa eksponenteng 1
examples.why: 'kapangyarihan' is 'power' in the might/authority sense, not the mathematical exponent; awkward for a technical reader though consistent and clear from context.
